title: Global warming and its impact on surface temperature trends in the Arctic region
abstract: This comprehensive study examines the accelerated warming patterns observed in the Arctic region over the past four decades. We analyze temperature anomalies and their correlation with greenhouse gas concentrations.

title: Temperature rise and ecosystem collapse: A meta-analysis of biodiversity loss
abstract: This meta-analysis synthesizes data from 500+ studies examining the relationship between global temperature increases and biodiversity loss. We found that for every 1°C rise in temperature, species loss increases exponentially.

title: Ocean warming and its consequences: Sea level rise and coastal vulnerability
abstract: Ocean temperatures have increased by 0.13°C per decade over the past 100 years, causing thermal expansion and accelerated ice sheet melting. This study projects sea level rise scenarios for the next century.

title: Mechanisms of climate warming: Radiative forcing and feedback loops
abstract: This review article examines the fundamental physics of greenhouse gas-induced warming, including radiative forcing calculations and positive feedback mechanisms such as ice-albedo feedback.

title: Global surface temperature records: Methods, uncertainties, and trends
abstract: We compare five independent global surface temperature datasets and assess their methodological differences and uncertainties. All datasets show a consistent warming trend of approximately 0.18°C per decade.
